title: IIT Hyderabad Post-Doctoral Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
organization: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IIT Hyderabad)
short_info: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad (IIT Hyderabad) Post-Doctoral Research Fellow Notification 2026 has been released on the official website iith.ac.in. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the IIT Hyderabad Post-Doctoral Research Fellow Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: Ph.D. in Chemical Engineering, Extensive experience with Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) research
qualification.details: Ph.D. in Chemical Engineering with extensive experience in Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) research.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Post-Doctoral Research Fellow (Tailing Coal to Coal Slurry)

salary_details.details: Rs. 70,680 to Rs. 84,500 per month (including HRA)

selection_process: academic record and relevant experience Interview for shortlisted candidates
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for IIT Hyderabad Post-Doctoral Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 30-05-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the IIT Hyderabad Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your email application for the Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position at IIT Hyderabad is 30th May 2026. Ensure your application reaches before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for this role?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Ph.D. in Chemical Engineering and have extensive experience in Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) research to be eligible for this Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position.

faq.question: Q3. How will candidates be selected for the Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The selection process involves initial shortlisting based on eligibility, academic records, and relevant experience. Shortlisted candidates will then be invited for an interview.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for the IIT Hyderabad Post-Doctoral Research Fellow recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive, making it accessible to all eligible candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a monthly salary ranging from Rs. 70,680 to Rs. 84,500, which includes House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q6. How should I apply for the Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: Applications should be submitted via email. You need to prepare your bio-data, attach necessary documents, and send it to the official email address specified in the notification before the last date.

faq.question: Q7. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position at IIT Hyderabad as per the released notification.

faq.question: Q8. What is the job type for this position?
faq.answer: The Post-Doctoral Research Fellow position at IIT Hyderabad is a contractual role, offering a fixed-term engagement.

faq.question: Q9. Where is IIT Hyderabad located?
faq.answer: Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad is located in Hyderabad, Telangana.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates from any state apply for this position?
faq.answer: While the institute is located in Telangana, the notification does not specify any state-wise restrictions for applicants, implying that candidates from all over India can apply if they meet the eligibility criteria.
